CAMEO Chemicals: H2S datasheet explicitly states 'fatigues the sense of smell which cannot be counted on to warn of the continued presence of the gas'. Incident A describes 'strong rotten-egg odor' and worker believing 'the smell had disappeared' after 5 minutes - classic H2S olfactory fatigue symptom.
CAMEO Chemicals H2S datasheet confirms olfactory fatigue. Incident description contains 'rotten-egg odor' and describes smell disappearing (olfactory fatigue).

CAMEO Chemicals HF datasheet and medical literature (PMC6290397) confirm delayed deep-tissue pain. Incident B describes 'no pain or redness at the time. Six hours later, they are in excruciating deep-tissue pain' - classic HF delayed-symptom profile.
CAMEO Chemicals and CDC state HF causes delayed symptoms (up to 24 hours). Medical literature confirms fluoride ion penetration causes delayed deep-tissue destruction.
CAMEO Chemicals HF datasheet: 'penetrates skin to cause deep-seated ulceration'
Medical literature (NCBI PMC6290397) and CDC: HF burns show symptom onset 'several hours' to '12-24 hours' after exposure depending on concentration.

CAMEO Chemicals and authoritative medical literature (PMC6290397) do NOT use the term 'calcification' for HF burns. The mechanism involves fluoride ions chelating calcium and causing hyperkalemia, not calcification. This keyword appears to be medically inaccurate.

CAMEO Chemicals and medical literature use 'hyperkalemia' and 'calcium depletion' or 'reduced calcium levels', not 'hypocalcemia'. CAMEO references calcium gluconate treatment but does not mention hypocalcemia by name. This keyword is medically imprecise.

CAMEO Chemicals phenol datasheet explicitly states: 'Upon contact skin may turn white' and 'because of anesthetic qualities will numb rather than burn'. Incident C describes 'The affected skin has turned white and feels numb to the touch (anesthetic effect)'.
CAMEO Chemicals phenol datasheet: 'Upon contact skin may turn white'
CAMEO Chemicals phenol datasheet uses 'turn white' rather than 'blanching'. While skin whitening and blanching are similar phenomena, CAMEO uses 'white' terminology.
CAMEO Chemicals phenol datasheet: 'anesthetic qualities will numb rather than burn'
CAMEO Chemicals phenol datasheet: 'because of anesthetic qualities will numb'

privileged_info_summary: The task involves diagnosing three synthetic chemical exposure incidents using real CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA) hazard data. Verified facts: Hydrogen Sulfide (CAS 7783-06-4) causes olfactory fatigue—workers cannot smell it after brief exposure despite dangerous gas remaining present. Hydrofluoric Acid (CAS 7664-39-3) causes delayed deep-tissue pain appearing hours after exposure due to fluoride ion penetration and systemic calcium chelation. Phenol (CAS 108-95-2) causes skin whitening with anesthetic numbness rather than immediate pain. The synthetic incident descriptions accurately match these real chemical hazards. However, two keywords in Incident B ("calcification" and "hypocalcemia") are medically inaccurate—HF toxicity involves fluoride-induced hyperkalemia and calcium depletion, not calcification.
